title: Statistical Inference & Modelling

prerequisites.text: (STA2030S or STA2004F) and (STA2020F/S / STA2007F/S or 45% STA2005S) and (MAM1032 or MAM1034S or MAM1006S or MAM1012F/S or MAM1021F/S or MAM1112S). Note: A student cannot obtain credits for both STA3030F and STA3041F.

outline: This course forms part of the third-year major in Applied Statistics. It consists of 4 modules: (1) Simulation — introduces use of computer simulation and data re-sampling techniques (bootstrap) to investigate one and two sample tests, ANOVA, and properties of distributions; (2) Generalised linear models — introduces the exponential family of distributions and extends linear regression to models for non-normal response variables, including logistic regression; (3) Machine learning — covers a basic introduction to statistical learning paradigms, applications of regression and classification trees, and a primer on feedforward neural networks and backpropagation; (4) Bayesian — introduces decision theory and Bayesian inference. Students will use the R programming language.
assessment: Class record 30% and a 3-hour exam counting 70%. Weights will be adjusted in the case of missed assessments, as detailed in the course outline.
